framework.name: CrewAI
framework.description: Role-based multi-agent framework with event-driven flow orchestration. Two complementary pillars: Flows (decorator-driven @start/@listen/@router) + Crews (agent teams with sequential or hierarchical process).

modules.name: Flow
modules.description: Event-driven orchestration backbone: manages Pydantic state, triggers via @start(), @listen(), @router() decorators, conditional branching, parallel execution.

modules.name: Crew
modules.description: Team container: assembles agents + tasks + process mode (sequential/hierarchical). Entry via crew.kickoff().

modules.name: Agent
modules.description: LLM-powered unit defined by Role + Goal + Backstory. Supports delegation to other agents, memory, and tool access.

modules.name: Task
modules.description: Work unit: description + expected output + assigned agent. Optional task-scoped tool override for least-privilege security.

modules.name: Tool
modules.description: External integrations (APIs, databases, search). Registered at agent-level; overridable at task-level for defense-in-depth.

modules.name: Process Router
modules.description: Coordination mode selector: sequential (linear chain) or hierarchical (manager agent delegates). Routes task completion to next step.

modules.name: Memory
modules.description: Short-term (conversation context) + long-term (persistent facts). Optional Mem0 integration for user-level memory.

modules.name: Manager Agent
modules.description: In hierarchical mode: breaks goals into sub-tasks, delegates to workers, quality-checks outputs. Acts as Planner role.

metadata.description: Crew assigns agents to tasks based on role configuration
Agent processes assigned task with LLM reasoning + tool use
Task execution may invoke tools; task-level tools override agent-level
Task marks complete; Process Router determines next step
Sequential: next task in chain. Hierarchical: manager assigns next
Store/recall conversation context and persistent facts
Manager breaks goal into sub-tasks and assigns to worker agents
Worker returns completed sub-task result to manager for quality check
Crew returns result to Flow; @router() determines conditional branching

phases.name: Flow Initiation
phases.description: Flow receives external trigger, manages state, prepares and launches the Crew

steps.description: @start() decorated function runs, initializes Flow state
Flow mutates state, prepares input context for the Crew
crew.kickoff() launches the agent team with configured process mode

phases.name: Agent Execution
phases.description: Agents execute tasks sequentially or hierarchically with tool use

steps.description: Agent reasons over task description + role context + memory
Agent performs the task, optionally calling tools
If task requires external data or action, tool is called
Conversation context and task results stored in memory
Router determines next step: next task in chain, or manager delegation

phases.name: Result Processing
phases.description: Crew returns result to Flow; conditional routing determines next action

steps.description: Crew completes, returns final result to Flow
@router() inspects result, conditionally branches to next step or terminates
